source_jobs.title: Immigration Attorney
source_jobs.description:
Immigration Attorney – Individual Practice



📍 Remote (U.S.-based) | Full-Time | $130K–$250K

Hiring at all experience levels — Associate through Senior



Whether you're a few years in or a decade deep, this role is built for attorneys who are serious about their craft and serious about where they're going.



Manifest Law is hiring Immigration Attorneys for our individual practice at all levels. You'll work on individual immigration matters — O-1, EB-1A, EB-2 NIW, H-1B, family-based, marriage-based — with caseload complexity and autonomy that scales with your experience. What doesn't scale is the standard.



What makes this different:

At most firms, you spend a significant portion of your time on coordination — chasing documents, following up on emails, tracking where a case stands across six different threads. At Manifest, the platform handles that:

 - Automated workflows manage attorney ↔ paralegal ↔ client handoffs so you're never the bottleneck

 - Smart draft tracking evaluates your filings against quality benchmarks and decision patterns — you get a feedback loop that actually makes you better

 - Unified context hub pulls every email, meeting note, and portal update into one place — full case context, always, without digging.

 - You practice law. The platform handles the rest.
   
   

How the model works:

You set your own caseload intensity. Take a focused, selective docket and earn a strong, stable income. Run an aggressive docket and your bonus grows with you. The lever is yours — with one condition: every case you take is worked at Manifest's standard. Aggressive SLAs, rigorous legal quality, client communication that earns loyalty. Volume never substitutes for quality here.



The career path is explicit:

 - Associates build real legal skills on real cases, with direct mentorship from senior attorneys

 - Mid-level attorneys grow into full case ownership and develop their client instincts

 - Senior attorneys operate with full autonomy, mentor junior colleagues, and build toward practice leadership

 - The destination, for attorneys who want it, is Practice Ownership — your own team, your own book, backed by the platform — earning $200K–$1M+



What we're looking for, at every level:

Associate (0–3 years): Strong legal research and writing, genuine interest in immigration law, high responsiveness, and the ambition to develop fast. Immigration clinic, paralegal, or externship background is a plus.

Mid-Level (3–6 years): Experience with employment-based or extraordinary ability petitions, growing comfort with direct client communication, and the drive to take real ownership of your matters.

Senior (6+ years): Deep expertise in O-1, EB-1A, and/or EB-2 NIW strategy, the ability to mentor junior attorneys, and the ambition to grow toward practice leadership — not just maintain a steady docket.



Across all levels, we require:

 - J.D. and active U.S. bar admission (or awaiting results for associates)

 - Full commitment to Manifest's SLAs and quality standards — non-negotiable at every level

 - Self-motivation and ownership mindset

 - A communication standard clients notice and remember
   
   

Compensation: $130,000–$250,000. Base scales with experience. Performance bonus scales with your output and quality. Path to $200K–$1M+ at practice leadership.

source_jobs.title: AI Native Immigration Paralegal
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT MANIFEST

Manifest is rebuilding the legal system from the ground up—starting with business immigration. We pair top attorneys with powerful software to help global talent live and work in the U.S. legally, quickly, and with confidence.

We’re backed by world-class investors and scaling fast. As we grow, we’re looking for experienced, detail-obsessed Paralegals to help us deliver a best-in-class client experience at scale.

This role is remote, and we welcome applicants from outside of the US. You can work from anywhere!

Pay is $15-25 per hour with a cap of a 40-hour workweek.




👩‍💼 THE ROLE

As a Paralegal at Manifest, you’ll be matched with one or more of our platform attorneys to provide strategic support across complex immigration casework. You’ll be a critical partner in the case lifecycle—from client intake to final filing.

This role goes beyond paperwork. You’ll drive document collection, draft persuasive application materials, and help clients navigate a process that can be both high-stakes and high-stress. You'll build long-term working relationships with attorneys who value your insight, precision, and reliability.




🔍 WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Document collection: Lead clients through the process of submitting required evidence—on time and in the right format.

 - Draft filings: Prepare USCIS forms, draft expert cover letters, compile exhibits, and assist in building persuasive narratives for EB1-A, EB2-NIW, and O-1 cases.

 - Client communication: Serve as a calming, confident point of contact—managing updates, answering questions, and maintaining strong relationships.

 - Case organization: Track deadlines, monitor progress, and ensure every case stays on rails.
   
   Attorney collaboration: Operate as a true partner—anticipating needs, aligning on preferences, and freeing attorneys to focus on legal strategy.
   
   


✅ REQUIREMENTS

 - 3+ years of experience in U.S. business immigration, with a strong focus on Exceptional Ability visas (EB1-A, EB2-NIW, and O-1)

 - Proven track record of owning paralegal workflows from intake through filing

 - Exceptional attention to detail and written communication skills

 - Experience communicating directly with clients, ideally in a high-volume or fast-paced setting

 - Highly organized, with a knack for juggling multiple deadlines

 - Comfort with legal tech and a growth mindset—you're excited to work differently, not just harder

 - Bonus: Experience working in a remote-first team or startup environment




💎 WHY JOIN MANIFEST

 - Join a high-performing team reimagining how immigration law gets done

 - Build deep relationships with attorneys who treat you as a strategic partner

 - Work remotely, flexibly, and with autonomy

 - Be part of a mission-driven company helping world-class talent live and work in the U.S.




🚀 READY TO HELP REDEFINE LEGAL WORK?

We’re not looking for case processors - we’re looking for trusted partners. If you’re passionate about immigration law, hungry to grow, and excited to do your best work in a modern, tech-enabled environment, we want to hear from you.

source_jobs.title: AI Native Corporate Immigration Paralegal
source_jobs.description:
About Manifest

Manifest is rebuilding the legal system from the ground up—starting with business immigration. We pair top attorneys with powerful software to help global talent live and work in the U.S. legally, quickly, and with confidence.

We’re backed by world-class investors and scaling fast. As we grow, we’re looking for experienced, detail-obsessed Paralegals to help us deliver a best-in-class client experience at scale.

This role is remote, and we welcome applicants from outside of the US. You can work from anywhere!

Pay is $15-25 per hour.



The Role

As a Corporate Practice Immigration Paralegal at Manifest, you’ll partner closely with one of our attorneys to execute high-volume, high-quality immigration work for corporate clients. You’ll manage a substantial caseload, drive end-to-end case preparation, and serve as a confident, reliable point of contact for HR teams and employees.

You should bring deep hands-on experience across PERMs and H-1Bs, with additional exposure to L-1s, E visas, and extraordinary ability cases preferred. You’ll be trusted to own workflow, communicate directly with corporate stakeholders, and deliver consistent accuracy in a fast-moving environment.



What You’ll Do

 - Case preparation: Lead PERM and H-1B processes from intake to filing, coordinating closely with HR, managers, and foreign nationals. Support additional nonimmigrant and immigrant categories such as L-1, E-1/E-2, and extraordinary ability visas (EB-1A, O-1, EB-2 NIW) as needed.

 - Document collection & evidence gathering: Guide clients through the process of gathering required evidence, clearly, efficiently, and with strong follow-through. Ensure all supporting documents are complete, accurate, and compliant with USCIS and DOL requirements.

 - Drafting & case assembly: Prepare USCIS forms, and draft clean, persuasive, well-organized case materials tailored to each petition type.

 - Client communication: Serve as a professional, friendly, and steady point of contact for corporate HR partners and foreign national employees. Handle updates, expectations-setting, and process explanations with clarity and confidence.

 - Case management & organization: Track deadlines, manage timelines, and keep a large volume of matters moving simultaneously. Maintain meticulous records and ensure nothing falls through the cracks.

 - Attorney partnership: Work closely with your supervising attorney, anticipating needs, aligning on preferences, and enabling them to focus on higher-level legal strategy.
   
   

Requirements

 - 3+ years of experience in U.S. corporate immigration, including significant hands-on experience with PERMs and H-1Bs

 - Experience managing high-volume corporate workloads and working directly with HR stakeholders

 - Comfort owning paralegal workflows from intake through filing

 - Strong drafting skills and exceptional attention to detail

 - Track record of direct client communication in a fast-paced or high-volume environment

 - High level of organization, reliability, and ability to juggle multiple deadlines smoothly

 - Comfort with legal technology, case management systems, and process improvements

 - A proactive, problem-solving mindset

 - Bonus: Experience with L-1s, E-1/E-2, EB-1A, O-1, or EB-2 NIW cases



Why Join Manifest

 - Join a high-performing team reimagining how immigration law gets done

 - Build deep relationships with attorneys who treat you as a strategic partner

 - Work remotely, flexibly, and with autonomy

 - Be part of a mission-driven company helping world-class talent live and work in the U.S.



Ready to Help Redefine Legal Work?

We’re not looking for case processors - we’re looking for trusted partners. If you’re passionate about immigration law, hungry to grow, and excited to do your best work in a modern, tech-enabled environment, we want to hear from you.

source_jobs.title: Corporate Practice Immigration Attorney
source_jobs.description:
Immigration Attorney – Corporate Practice

📍 Remote (U.S.-based) | Full-Time | $130K–$250K

Hiring at all experience levels — Associate through Senior

Corporate immigration done right is genuinely complex work. Manifest Law is hiring attorneys for our employer practice at every level — from associates learning the craft to senior attorneys running full client portfolios and building toward practice leadership.



What you'll work on:

The full employer immigration spectrum — H-1B, L-1, O-1, TN, PERM, EB-2/EB-3 green cards, E-1/E-2 — for companies ranging from funded startups to large enterprises. Complexity and autonomy scale with your experience. The standard doesn't.



What makes this different:

At most firms, attorneys spend a significant portion of their time on coordination rather than legal work. At Manifest, the platform handles that:

 - Automated workflows manage attorney ↔ paralegal ↔ client handoffs so nothing falls through and you're never chasing status

 - Smart draft tracking benchmarks your filings and builds a feedback loop — you get better with every case

 - Unified context hub consolidates every email, meeting note, and portal update into one place per matter — full case context, always, without digging through inboxes

You practice law. The platform handles the rest.



How the model works:

You control your caseload intensity. A focused, selective portfolio earns a strong, stable income. A more aggressive one earns significantly above market. The lever is yours — with one condition: every client you take on gets Manifest's standard. Aggressive SLAs, rigorous legal work, and the proactive communication that makes HR teams trust their outside counsel.



The career path is explicit:

 - Associates build hands-on experience on real employer matters with direct mentorship

 - Mid-level attorneys grow into full client ownership and strategic advisor skills

 - Senior attorneys operate autonomously, mentor junior colleagues, and develop toward practice leadership

 - The destination, for those who want it, is Practice Ownership — earning $200K–$1M+
   
   

What we're looking for, at every level:

Associate (0–3 years): Strong research and writing skills, organized and responsive work style, genuine interest in employer immigration. Paralegal, clinic, or corporate law background a plus.

Mid-Level (3–6 years): Hands-on H-1B, PERM, or intracompany transfer experience; comfort working directly with HR contacts; proactive ownership approach.

Senior (6+ years): Deep expertise across the employer immigration spectrum; ability to serve as a trusted strategic advisor to client HR and Legal leadership; drive toward practice leadership.



Across all levels:

 - J.D. and active U.S. bar admission (or awaiting results for associates)

 - Full commitment to Manifest's SLAs and quality standards

 - Self-motivated, organized, deadline-driven

 - Communication that client-side HR teams find clear, proactive, and trustworthy
   
   

Compensation: $130,000–$250,000. Base scales with experience. Bonus scales with your output and quality. Path to $200K–$1M+ at practice leadership.

source_jobs.title: Immigration Strategy Attorney (Consultations Only)
source_jobs.description:
Manifest is rebuilding the legal system from the ground up—starting with business immigration. We pair top attorneys with powerful software to help global talent live and work in the U.S. legally, quickly, and with confidence.

We’re backed by world-class investors and scaling fast. As we grow, we’re looking for experienced, detail-obsessed Attorneys to help us deliver a best-in-class client experience at scale.

This role is remote, and we welcome applicants from outside of the US. You can work from anywhere!

Salary range: $140-190K



👩 💼 The Role

At Manifest, the Immigration Strategy Attorney is the front line of the prospective client experience. You’ll be the first Manifest attorney that prospective clients speak to, and your job is to ensure every consultation is handled with warmth, clarity, speed, and precision. Consultations will primarily focus on extraordinary ability and high-skilled categories such as EB-1A, EB-2 NIW, and O-1A, but may also include inquiries across a range of other immigration categories, breaking news, and fast-changing policies.

You’ll conduct consultations with prospective clients from around the world, evaluate their eligibility and overall case strength, and provide clear recommendations on the right next step. You’ll be responsible for identifying strong candidates quickly, managing expectations for borderline cases, and diplomatically explaining when a client is not a fit, all while making clients feel understood, supported, and informed.

This role is ideal for an attorney who is personable, confident speaking with clients, and thrives in a high-volume environment where fast, accurate legal assessment and strong communication are essential.



🔍 What You’ll Do

Client consultations & first legal contact

 - Conduct consultations with prospective clients and provide clear, confident explanations of Manifest’s services and process.

 - Evaluate prospective clients for extraordinary ability and high-skilled immigration pathways, including EB-1A, EB-2 NIW, O-1A, and related categories.

 - Communicate in a way that feels calm, professional, and supportive, even when clients are anxious or unfamiliar with immigration terminology.

 - Explain complex legal concepts in simple, accessible language to clients from diverse backgrounds and countries.



Case evaluation & qualification

 - Review client information during consultations, assess viability, and classify case strength (e.g., strong / moderate / weak / unclear).

 - Identify key indicators of high potential (e.g., strong publications, awards, high-profile work, strong salary, press, critical roles, membership, judging, original contributions).

 - Identify common red flags, missing information, credibility issues, or factors that weaken case strategy.

 - Provide clear next-step guidance, including whether a client should proceed, reposition, or not move forward.

 - Make strong, fast judgment calls on fit and communicate decisions with clarity and empathy.



Documentation + consultation notes

 - Capture clear consultation notes and recommendations in the firm’s systems.

 - Ensure lead data is complete and consistent for follow-up and downstream workflow.

 - Maintain clean records so nothing falls through the cracks.

 - Track and flag high-priority candidates or time-sensitive cases.



✅ Requirements

 - 5+ years of law practice experience, with significant exposure to U.S. business immigration.

 - Strong working knowledge of extraordinary ability and high-skilled categories, including EB-1A, EB-2 NIW, O-1A, and related strategies.

 - Fast learner. You keep up with changing government policies and can quickly identify a proposed policy’s or regulation’s potential real-life impacts to applicants.

 - Deep comfort assessing candidacy quickly and accurately across a wide range of professional profiles.

 - Exceptional communication skills. You are crisp, warm, confident, and highly articulate.

 - Strong judgment and triage ability

source_jobs.title: Immigration Attorney, Practice Owner
source_jobs.description:
📍 Remote (U.S.-based) | Full-Time | $200K–$1M+

You've built the practice. You know the work. The thing holding you back isn't skill or ambition — it's the operational ceiling that comes with running a firm: the overhead, the hiring, the marketing, the technology, the business development grind that pulls you away from the legal work and the growth you're actually here for.

Manifest Law is a tech-enabled immigration firm with 3,000+ successful cases. We're looking for an experienced individual immigration attorney — including those already running their own firm — who wants to scale further and faster than they could alone, without the operational drag that caps most practices.

The platform we provide:

 - A base salary that protects your floor as you scale

 - An inbound client pipeline through Manifest's marketing and BD engine — cases come to you

 - Purpose-built technology that handles the work between the legal work:
   
   - Automated workflows coordinating attorney ↔ paralegal ↔ client so nothing falls through the cracks
   
   - Smart draft review that tracks quality across filings and builds a feedback loop over time
   
   - Unified context hub aggregating emails, meetings, and portal activity so you and your team always know exactly where every case stands

 - Paralegal and operational support — so your attorneys practice law, not project management

The comp structure:

Manifest-sourced = Base + profit share on team performance

Self-sourced = Base + higher profit share — your book, your upside

The more you've built, the more you benefit from bringing it. Your existing clients and referral network earn at the higher rate from day one.

What this role demands:

This is not a heads-down legal role. You will be the public face of your practice — featured in Manifest's content, on social, in client-facing materials, and in the press. The best Practice Owners are attorneys clients want to hire by name. If you've already built a reputation, Manifest amplifies it.

You'll also lead and develop a team. Running case reviews, holding a high standard, and building the next generation of Manifest lawyers is core to this role.

What we're looking for:

 - J.D. and active U.S. bar admission

 - 7+ years of immigration experience with a strong individual/extraordinary ability portfolio

 - Experience managing or mentoring legal teams

 - Comfort with client-facing visibility — thought leadership, social presence, media

 - A professional network that generates referrals

 - Genuine entrepreneurial ambition — you want to build, not just practice

The floor:

Every Manifest attorney is held to aggressive response time SLAs, rigorous legal quality standards, and high client satisfaction requirements. This is non-negotiable regardless of case source or caseload volume — and it's why clients pay us a premium.

Compensation: $200K–$1M+. Base protects your floor. Profit share — higher on self-sourced, standard on Manifest-sourced — is where the ceiling opens up.

source_jobs.title: Corporation Immigration Attorney, Practice Owner
source_jobs.description:
📍 Remote (U.S.-based) | Full-Time | $200K–$1M+

You've spent years managing employer immigration programs — H-1Bs, PERMs, E-1/E-2s, intracompany transfers, global mobility — for companies that depend on you. You've probably thought about what it would look like to build your own practice. The thing that stops most attorneys is everything that isn't the legal work.

Manifest Law removes all of it.

We're hiring a Corporate Immigration Practice Owner — an experienced employer-side attorney ready to scale their practice further and faster than they could alone, backed by Manifest's technology, business development engine, and full operational infrastructure.

The platform we provide:

 - A base salary while your practice scales — you're not building on risk

 - Inbound corporate client pipeline from Manifest's BD and marketing engine

 - Purpose-built technology that eliminates the work between the legal work:
   
   - Automated workflows coordinating attorney ↔ paralegal ↔ client across every matter
   
   - Smart draft review benchmarking filings against quality standards and decision patterns with a built-in feedback loop
   
   - Unified context hub consolidating emails, meetings, and portal activity so your team always knows exactly where every case stands

 - Paralegal and operational support — your attorneys practice law, not project management

The comp structure:

Manifest-sourced = Base + profit share on team performance

Self-sourced = Base + higher profit share — your book, your upside

Companies you bring through your own network earn a meaningfully higher profit percentage from day one.

What this role demands:

You are the face of the corporate immigration practice — featured in Manifest's content, on social, at events, and in client-facing presentations. HR and Legal teams at your clients should know your name and want to call you. The best Practice Owners are attorneys companies retain because they trust that attorney specifically.

You'll also lead a team. Building, developing, and holding attorneys to Manifest's standard is core to this role.

What we're looking for:

 - J.D. and active bar admission

 - 7+ years of employer-side immigration experience

 - Experience managing or developing legal teams

 - Comfort with public visibility — thought leadership, social, client events

 - A professional network in HR, Legal, or People Ops circles

 - The ambition to build a practice, not just run a docket

The floor:

Aggressive SLAs, rigorous legal quality, proactive client communication — non-negotiable regardless of case source or volume. This is the standard companies pay a premium for.

Compensation: $200K–$1M+. Base protects your floor. Profit share — higher on self-sourced, standard on Manifest-sourced — is where the ceiling opens up.

source_jobs.title: Contract Immigration Attorney
source_jobs.description:
Immigration Attorney – Individual Practice (Contract)

📍 Remote (U.S.-based) | Contract / 1099 | Pay Per Case | $200K–$300K+ potential

Minimum 4 years' experience as a practicing attorney

This role is built for attorneys who are serious about their craft and serious about what they earn — with the experience to take real ownership from day one.

Manifest Law is engaging contract Immigration Attorneys for our individual practice. You'll work on individual immigration matters — O-1, EB-1A, EB-2 NIW, H-1B, family-based, marriage-based — with caseload complexity and autonomy that scales with your experience. You're paid per case, not per hour or per year. What doesn't scale is the standard.

What makes this different:
At most firms, you spend a significant portion of your time on coordination — chasing documents, following up on emails, tracking where a case stands across six different threads. That's unbilled time you never get back. At Manifest, the platform handles that:

 - Automated workflows manage attorney ↔ paralegal ↔ client handoffs so you're never the bottleneck

 - Smart draft tracking evaluates your filings against quality benchmarks and decision patterns — you get a feedback loop that actually makes you better

 - Unified context hub pulls every email, meeting note, and portal update into one place — full case context, always, without digging.

 - You practice law. The platform handles the rest — so every hour you put in goes toward billable, case-advancing work.

How the model works:
This is pay-per-case. You set your own caseload intensity, and your earnings move with it. Take a focused, selective docket and earn a strong, predictable per-case income. Run an aggressive docket and your earnings climb fast — attorneys working at volume can reach $200K–$300K+. The lever is yours — with one condition: every case you take is worked at Manifest's standard. Aggressive SLAs, rigorous legal quality, client communication that earns loyalty. Volume never substitutes for quality here.

The path is explicit:

 - Experienced attorneys grow into full case ownership and sharpen their client instincts

 - Senior attorneys operate with full autonomy, take on higher-complexity (higher-fee) matters, and mentor colleagues

 - The destination, for attorneys who want it, is Practice Ownership — your own team, your own book, backed by the platform — earning $200K–$1M+

What we're looking for:
Mid-Level (4–6 years): Experience with employment-based or extraordinary ability petitions, comfort with direct client communication, and the drive to take real ownership of your matters.
Senior (6+ years): Deep expertise in O-1, EB-1A, and/or EB-2 NIW strategy, the ability to mentor junior attorneys, and the appetite to run a high-volume, high-quality docket.

Across all levels, we require:

 - Minimum 4 years' experience as a practicing attorney

 - J.D. and active U.S. bar admission

 - Full commitment to Manifest's SLAs and quality standards — non-negotiable

 - Self-motivation and ownership mindset

 - A communication standard clients notice and remember

 - Ability to work as an independent contractor (1099)

Compensation: Pay per case. Per-matter fees scale with complexity and experience. No cap — your earnings track your output and quality.

notes: Manifest Law is a tech-enabled immigration firm with a focus on hiring attorneys and paralegals for various roles.
